backend/agent/pipeline.py::_pick_best_pa.docstring:
When multiple patches target the same file, pick the most complete one.

    Based on SWE-Agent research: one edit per file per turn prevents cascading conflicts.
    Selection criteria: longest patched_code that actually applies to the source.

backend/agent/pipeline.py::repair_node.name: repair_node
backend/agent/pipeline.py::repair_node.docstring:
Stage 4: Agentic repair with per-file verification and sequential patching.

    Architecture (based on SWE-Agent/Agentless best practices):
    1. Generate all patches in one call (overview of the fix)
    2. Deduplicate overlapping patches for the same file
    3. Apply patches sequentially per file (prevents conflicts)
    4. Verify each patch against actual source on disk
    5. If patches fail to match, re-read the file and retry
    6. If LLM needs more files, read them and re-run
    7. Syntax-check is done in test_node before commit

backend/analyzer/call_graph.py.name: call_graph.py
backend/analyzer/call_graph.py.docstring:
call_graph.py — Build a directed call/import/containment graph from ParsedFile data.

Graph node IDs follow the convention:
  - file      : "<rel_path>"
  - function  : "<rel_path>::<func_name>"
  - method    : "<rel_path>::<class_name>::<method_name>"
  - class     : "<rel_path>::<class_name>"

Edge types:
  CONTAINS  — structural containment (file→class, file→function, class→method)
  IMPORTS   — file A imports from file B (resolved best-effort)
  CALLS     — function/method body references another known callable
  INHERITS  — class B inherits from class A

backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::_resolve.name: _resolve_import_module
backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::_resolve.docstring:
Try to map an import module string to a relative file path present in known_paths.

Handles:
- Absolute imports: "a.b.c" → "a/b/c.py" or "a/b/c/__init__.py"
- Relative imports: ".sibling" or "..parent.sibling"

backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::_scan_bo.name: _scan_body_for_calls
backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::_scan_bo.docstring:
Scan raw source text for function call patterns that match known callable names.
Returns a list of target node IDs.

Uses receiver-qualified matching to avoid name collisions:
- `self.method()` → match methods in the same class only
- `variable.method()` → match methods in the class the variable was imported as
- `ClassName(...)` → match class constructors
- `bare_function()` → match functions in the same file first, then others

The file_imports dict maps alias/variable names to their resolved file path,
e.g. {"family_service": "app/service/family_service.py"}.

backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::_slice_l.name: _slice_lines
backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::_slice_l.docstring: Return lines [start, end] (1-indexed, inclusive) joined as a string.

backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::CallGrap.name: CallGraphBuilder
backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::CallGrap.docstring:
Build a call / import / containment graph from a list of ParsedFile dicts
(as produced by CodeParser.parse_all()).

Usage::

    from backend.analyzer.code_parser import CodeParser
    from backend.analyzer.call_graph import CallGraphBuilder

    parsed = CodeParser(repo_path).parse_all()
    graph_data = CallGraphBuilder(parsed).build()

backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::CallGrap.name: _add_call_edges
backend/analyzer/call_graph.py::CallGrap.docstring:
Best-effort: re-parse function body source text to find identifier
references to known callables.

Uses receiver-qualified matching to avoid phantom edges from name collisions.
E.g., `service.create_family()` only links to the service's method, not
to every class that has a `create_family` method.

backend/analyzer/git_analyzer.py::GitAna.name: analyze
backend/analyzer/git_analyzer.py::GitAna.docstring:
Perform a git history analysis.

Returns
-------
dict
    A dictionary containing:
    - change_frequency: {file_path: commit_count} for every file
      that appears in the commit history
    - recent_changes: list of the last RECENT_COMMITS_LIMIT commits,
      each as {hash, message, date, files_changed}
    - hotspot_files: top HOTSPOT_LIMIT files by commit count,
      as a sorted list of {path, commits}

If the directory is not a git repository, or has no history, or git
is unavailable, all values are returned as empty collections.

        ``"code"`` → File, Class, Function nodes;
        ``"business"`` → BusinessRule, DomainConcept nodes;
        ``None`` → all node types.
    node_type:
        Exact Neo4j label to filter on, e.g. ``"File"``.  Takes precedence
        over *layer* when both are supplied.
    limit:
